title: Data flow programming of computing apparatus with vector estimation-based graph partitioning
abstract: In various embodiments, a spectral graph partitioner ("SP") of a graph partitioning system ("GPS") may partition a data flow graph associated with a program into a plurality of subgraphs to be used to perform analysis or debugging. The SP may generate estimated eigenvectors for a matrix representing the graph through minimization of a function on the vectors. The SP may generate multiple eigenvectors to perform the clustering in a multi-dimensional space described by the eigenvectors. The SP may refine the clustering by repeating generation of eigenvectors to describe higher-dimensional spaces and perform further clustering. The SP may also determine quality metrics for the clusters and may stop refinement based on the quality metrics. The GPS may select between utilizing the SP or utilizing one or more other partitioners based on various factors such as, for example, graph size or quality metrics.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A computing apparatus equipped to support data flow programming, comprising: one or more computing processors; and a spectral graph partitioner to operate on the one or more computing processors to partition a data flow graph associated with a program into a plurality of subgraphs; wherein the spectral graph petitioner includes: a vector estimator to generate one or more estimated vectors to partition the graph into the plurality of subgraphs; and a cluster determiner to : define a multi-dimensional space based on the plurality of estimated vectors; and cluster nodes of the graph to form the plurality of subgraphs in the multi-dimensional space; and wherein the plurality of subgraphs are used in analysis or debugging of the program, and the program is used to program the computing apparatus or another computing apparatus to perform a task. 2. The computing apparatus of claim 1, wherein the vector estimator is to generate the one or more estimated vectors through generation of eigenvectors for a Laplacian matrix describing the graph. 3. The computing apparatus of claim 2, wherein the vector estimator is to generate an eigenvector as a vector v through minimization of a function described by / = âi~j(vi â VJY + ^ (nâ IMI 2 ) 2 , where n is a number of vertices in the graph and c is a constant. 4. The computing apparatus of claim 3, wherein minimization of the function comprises performance of a gradient descent on the function 5. The computing apparatus of claim 3, wherein a value of the constant c is based at least in part on and inverse of a degree of the graph. 6. The computing apparatus of claim 3, wherein generate eigenvectors comprises set one coordinate of the vector v to 0 prior to minimization of the function 7. The computing apparatus of claim 6, wherein generate eigenvectors comprises set, after minimization of the function the coordinate which had been set to 0 to an average of its coordinate neighbors. 8. The computing apparatus of any of claims 1-7, wherein the cluster determiner is to cluster the nodes of the graph to form the plurality of subgraphs through performance of a -means clustering process. 9. The computing apparatus of any of claims 1-7, wherein the spectral graph partitioner further comprises a quality metric determiner to determine, given a clustering of nodes of the graph, one or more quality metrics for the clustering of the nodes. 10. The computing apparatus of claim 9, wherein the quality metric determiner is to determine a modularity metric or a cluster path length metric for the clustering of the nodes. 11. The computing apparatus of claim 9, wherein the spectral graph partitioner further comprises a refinement controller to cause repetition of the generation of estimated vectors and the clustering of nodes of the graph based at least in part on the one or more quality metrics. 12. The computing apparatus of claim 11, wherein the refinement controller is to cause the nodes of the graph to be clustered into an increased numbers of clusters as additional estimated vectors are generated. 13. The computing apparatus of any of claims 1-7, further comprising a partitioner selector to select between utilizing the spectral graph partitioner or one or more other graph partitioners of the apparatus. 14. The apparatus of claim 13, wherein the partitioner selector is to select based at least in part on a size of the graph or one or more quality metrics determined for one or more clusterings generated by the spectral partitioner. 15. The apparatus of claim 13, wherein the partitioner selector is to select between the spectral partitioner and an edge centrality partitioner. 16. A computer-implemented method for supporting data flow programming, the method comprising: receiving, by a computing system, a data flow graph associated with a program; generating, by the computing system, one or more estimated vectors to partition the graph into a plurality of subgraphs; defining, by the computing system, a multi-dimensional space based on the plurality of estimated vectors; and clustering, by the computing system, nodes of the graph to form the plurality of subgraphs in the multi-dimensional space; wherein: 

 the plurality of subgraphs are used in analysis or debugging of the program; and the program is used to program the computing system or another computing system to perform a task. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein generating the one or more estimated vectors comprises generating eigenvectors for a Laplacian matrix describing the graph through (nâ |M| 2 ) 2 , where n is a number of vertices in the graph and c is a constant. 18. The method of any of claims 16 or 17, wherein clustering the nodes of the graph to form the plurality of subgraphs comprises performing a -means clustering process. 19. The method of any of claims 16 or 17, further comprising determining, by the computing system, given a clustering of nodes of the graph, one or more quality metrics for the clustering of the nodes. 20. The method of claim 19, further comprising causing, by the computing system, repetition of the generation of estimated vectors and the clustering of nodes of the graph based at least in part on the one or more quality metrics. 21. One or more computer-readable media containing instructions written thereon that, in response to execution on a computing system, cause the computing system to support data flow programming by causing the computing system to perform the method of any of claims 16 or 17. 22. A computing apparatus for supporting data flow programming, comprising: means for receiving a data flow graph associated with a program; means for generating one or more estimated vectors to partition the graph into a plurality of subgraphs; means for defining a multi-dimensional space based on the plurality of estimated vectors; and means for clustering nodes of the graph to form the plurality of subgraphs in the multi-dimensional space; wherein: the plurality of subgraphs are used in analysis or debugging of the program; and the program is used to program the computing apparatus or another computing apparatus to perform a task. 23. The computing apparatus of claim 22, wherein means for generating the one or more estimated vectors comprise means for generating eigenvectors for a Laplacian matrix describing the graph through minimizing a function described by / = âi~ 7 (i?iâ vj) + ^ (nâ IMI 2 ) 2 , where n is a number of vertices in the graph and c is a constant. 24. The computing apparatus of any of claims 22 or 23, wherein means for clustering the nodes of the graph to form the plurality of subgraphs comprise means for performing a -means clustering process. 25. The computing apparatus of any of claims 22 or 23, further comprising: means for determining, given a clustering of nodes of the graph, one or more quality metrics for the clustering of the nodes; and means for causing repetition of the generation of estimated vectors and the clustering of nodes of the graph based at least in part on the one or more quality metrics.
